item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's not a half-bad emulation of Josephus

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Definitely like the overall feel of a conglomeration of brass and clay and its conflicting personalities, although I do feel the Greek letters representing the phases of the golem were a little forced…

Still, I'll give it an upvote for giving me the image of a giant robot punching blindly XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like the Greek-letter phases. This thing is a mash-up of golems from different creators, it shouldn't keep a to-the-minute behavior schedule. Other than that I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since the collapsibles have been giving people trouble, I decided to remove them and used some red-text to give the impression that there was an in-universe collapsible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes. You can change the styling of them, but that's a pretty advanced application of wikidot code.

Adding the following:

[[module css]]
#page-content .collapsible-block {
max-width: 100%;
text-align: center;
text-color: black;
border-color: #b01;
border: solid 1px #b01;
background-color: #E6DD93;
}
.collapsible-block-content p {
text-align: left;
padding-left: 5px;
padding-right: 5px;
}
[[/module]]

Somewhere in the page will modify the Collapsibles to use the style I've used in several others.

I would suggest changing the background-color and border-color properties to fit your needs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you want visibility to be maximised, not using collapsibles is an option. This is mostly a choice on presentation, and it's up to you to decide (if it comes down to that).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there anything that I could do to help make the collapsibles easier to see, or…?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't feel like that. I also missed the damn collapsible ;w;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As far as the style goes, point taken. I think that "bade" fits in well, but "behold" is a little bit too forced.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This turned out slightly different from what I expected, but this version is clearly one borne from your decisions and I recognise that it is beneficial to the article regardless.

While it appears that a mere storm merged the golems of Judaism and Mekhanism into one, I can't help but feel something amiss. I think it is alright in this article not to address that particular elephant in the room, and leave that for speculation.

As for the Mehkanist text, I see that their dogma decides that they will blame others for the mishap at Ephesus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well written and an engaging story. 'Giant robot' isn't very original, but 'giant robot made when a lot of little robots melted together by accident' is. +1 especially for the programming being a mix of the two predecessors'.

One thing that could possibly use a little tweaking is the first collapsible. Can't put my finger on anything specific, but the Abrahamic-religious-text style feels ever so slightly forced. Perhaps there's one or two too many 'behold's or 'bade's.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nvm i missed the collapsble i feel so dumb

item.title: Thoughts and Thanks
item.content:encoded:
Because every author needs to take a shot at a Series I-style giant monster at some point, right?

My thanks to Updog and MrWrong, who read this draft as a draft on the forums.
